channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment to no longer release single-player games on PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5

Confirmed by Hermen Hulst during company town hall.

Sony Interactive Entertainment will no longer release its big single-player titles on PC, Sony Interactive Entertainment’s Studio Business Group CEO Hermen Hulst said in a company town hall today, via Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier.

The confirmation follows a Bloomberg report from March, in which sources claimed titles like Ghost of Yotei and Saros will remain exclusive to PlayStation 5 going forward, while online games such as Marathon and MARVEL Tokon: Fighting Souls will still be made available on PC.

Upcoming single-player titles from Sony Interactive Entertainment include God of War Trilogy Remake and Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, which will presumably also remain exclusive to console. External publishing deals like Kena: Scars of Kosmora, which will also be available for PC, do not seem to be affected.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/sony-interactive-entertainment-to-no-longer-release-single-player-games-on-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
